members.name: _GetMinimumSize
members.summary: Virtual method to be implemented by the user. Returns a custom minimum size that the stylebox must respect when drawing. By default [method get_minimum_size] only takes content margins into account. This method can be overridden to add another size restriction. A combination of the default behavior and the output of this method will be used, to account for both sizes.

members.name: Draw
members.signature: public void Draw(RID canvasItem, Rect2 rect)
members.summary: Draws this stylebox using a canvas item identified by the given [RID]. The [RID] value can either be the result of [method CanvasItem.get_canvas_item] called on an existing [CanvasItem]-derived node, or directly from creating a canvas item in the [RenderingServer] with [method RenderingServer.canvas_item_create].

members.name: GetContentMargin
members.summary: Returns the default margin of the specified [enum Side].

members.name: GetCurrentItemDrawn
members.summary: Returns the [CanvasItem] that handles its [constant CanvasItem.NOTIFICATION_DRAW] or [method CanvasItem._draw] callback at this moment.

members.name: GetMargin
members.summary: Returns the content margin offset for the specified [enum Side]. Positive values reduce size inwards, unlike [Control]'s margin values.

members.name: GetMinimumSize
members.summary: Returns the minimum size that this stylebox can be shrunk to.

members.name: GetOffset
members.summary: Returns the "offset" of a stylebox. This helper function returns a value equivalent to [code]Vector2(style.get_margin(MARGIN_LEFT), style.get_margin(MARGIN_TOP))[/code].

members.name: SetContentMargin
members.signature: public void SetContentMargin(int margin, float offset)
members.summary: Sets the default value of the specified [enum Side] to [param offset] pixels.

members.name: SetContentMarginAll
members.summary: Sets the default margin to [param offset] pixels for all sides.

members.name: TestMask
members.signature: public bool TestMask(Vector2 point, Rect2 rect)
members.summary: Test a position in a rectangle, return whether it passes the mask test.

members.name: ContentMarginBottom
members.summary: The bottom margin for the contents of this style box. Increasing this value reduces the space available to the contents from the bottom. If this value is negative, it is ignored and a child-specific margin is used instead. For example, for [StyleBoxFlat], the border thickness (if any) is used instead. It is up to the code using this style box to decide what these contents are: for example, a [Button] respects this content margin for the textual contents of the button. [method get_margin] should be used to fetch this value as consumer instead of reading these properties directly. This is because it correctly respects negative values and the fallback mentioned above.

members.name: ContentMarginLeft
members.summary: The left margin for the contents of this style box. Increasing this value reduces the space available to the contents from the left. Refer to [member content_margin_bottom] for extra considerations.

members.name: ContentMarginRight
members.summary: The right margin for the contents of this style box. Increasing this value reduces the space available to the contents from the right. Refer to [member content_margin_bottom] for extra considerations.

members.name: ContentMarginTop
members.summary: The top margin for the contents of this style box. Increasing this value reduces the space available to the contents from the top. Refer to [member content_margin_bottom] for extra considerations.

virtualMethods.name: _GetMinimumSize
virtualMethods.summary: Virtual method to be implemented by the user. Returns a custom minimum size that the stylebox must respect when drawing. By default [method get_minimum_size] only takes content margins into account. This method can be overridden to add another size restriction. A combination of the default behavior and the output of this method will be used, to account for both sizes.
